audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Exodus / Shemot tabernacle construction text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for artisan work, excessive freewill offering, curtains, coverings, frames, bars, veil, and screen.

verses.restored: Bezalel and Oholiab and every wise-hearted man in whom YHWH gave wisdom and understanding to know how to do all work of service of holy place shall do according to all YHWH commanded.
Moses called Bezalel and Oholiab and every wise-hearted man in whose heart YHWH gave wisdom, everyone whose heart lifted him to draw near to the work to do it.
They took from before Moses all contribution that children of Israel brought for work of service of holy place, to do it. And they still brought to him freewill offering morning by morning.
All wise ones doing all work of holy place came, each from his work that they were doing,
and said to Moses, The people are bringing more than enough for service of work that YHWH commanded to do.
Moses commanded, and they passed a voice in camp, saying, Man and woman shall not make any more work for contribution of holy place. So the people were restrained from bringing.
The work they had was sufficient for all work to do it, and more.
Every wise-hearted one among doers of work made dwelling-place with ten curtains of fine twisted linen, blue, purple, and scarlet, with cherubim, work of skilled designer.
Length of one curtain twenty-eight cubits, width four cubits; one measure for all curtains.
He joined five curtains one to another, and five curtains joined one to another.
He made loops of blue on edge of one curtain at end of set; thus he did on edge of outer curtain in second set.
Fifty loops he made in one curtain, and fifty loops on edge of curtain in second set; loops were opposite each other.
He made fifty gold clasps and joined curtains one to another with clasps, and dwelling-place became one.
He made curtains of goats' hair for tent over dwelling-place; eleven curtains he made.
Length of one curtain thirty cubits, width four cubits; one measure for eleven curtains.
He joined five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves.
He made fifty loops on edge of outer curtain of set, and fifty loops on edge of curtain of second set.
He made fifty bronze clasps to join tent to be one.
He made covering for tent of reddened ram skins and covering of durable hides above.
He made frames for dwelling-place of acacia wood, standing upright.
Ten cubits length of frame, and one and a half cubits width of one frame.
Two tenons for one frame, joined each to another; so he did for all frames of dwelling-place.
He made frames for dwelling-place: twenty frames for south side, toward south.
Forty silver bases he made under twenty frames, two bases under one frame for its two tenons and two bases under another frame for its two tenons.
For second side of dwelling-place, north side, he made twenty frames,
and their forty silver bases, two bases under one frame and two bases under another frame.
For rear of dwelling-place westward, he made six frames.
Two frames he made for corners of dwelling-place in rear.
They were doubled below and together complete at top into one ring; thus he did for both corners.
There were eight frames and their silver bases, sixteen bases, two bases under each frame.
He made bars of acacia wood, five for frames of one side of dwelling-place,
five bars for frames of second side of dwelling-place, and five bars for frames at rear westward.
He made middle bar to pass through midst of frames from end to end.
He overlaid frames with gold, made their rings of gold as housings for bars, and overlaid bars with gold.
He made veil of blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, with cherubim, work of skilled designer.
He made for it four pillars of acacia, overlaid them with gold, their hooks gold, and cast for them four silver bases.
He made screen for entrance of tent, blue, purple, scarlet, and fine twisted linen, work of embroiderer,
and its five pillars and their hooks, and overlaid their tops and bands with gold; their five bases were bronze.

translation_notes: Construction follows command pattern from Exodus 26 but now emphasizes actual wise-hearted labor.
Freewill offering exceeds need; Moses restrains giving. This is crucial anti-extraction safeguard.
Repeated one-ness of dwelling/tent is carried into construction.
safety_note: The people bring more than enough and are stopped from bringing more. Sacred work must not become endless fundraising or spiritualized extraction.
